GOV.UK GovWifi analysis

Administrator Onboarding Journey — Analysis

Date: 2026-07-08  ·  App: govwifi-admin (Rails 8)  ·  Branch: analysis/admin-onboarding-journey

The administrator sign-up / onboarding journey, walked end-to-end on a locally-run instance (localhost:8080) with a real account (Cabinet Office, gov.uk email, live two-factor auth). Every screen was captured and the source code traced to confirm behaviour, validation and data storage. All screenshots below are embedded in this file.

Contents
  1. How this was produced
  2. User journey map
  3. The journey, step by step (with screenshots)
  4. Data collected
  5. Compliance comparison
  6. Recommendations
  7. Low-fidelity wireframes

1. How this was produced

  • Built and served the app (MySQL 8.4 + Rails), reachable at http://localhost:8080.
  • Walked the journey by hand as a genuinely new user: signed up with a @gov.uk email, followed the Devise confirmation link, set a password and created the organisation, completed real two-factor auth using the TOTP secret shown on screen, reached "invite second admin", skipped it, and landed on Locations/Settings.
  • The banner reads "production" because SITE_CONFIG defaults that label in dev; this is a local instance. A dark band in some captures is the local rack-mini-profiler debug bar (development only).

2. User journey map

                         ADMINISTRATOR ONBOARDING — GovWifi Admin

  [ Landing / Sign in ]                                            (unauthenticated)
        |  "Sign up for a GovWifi administrator account"
        v
  +---------------------+   email must match gov/public-sector regexp (allowlist from S3)
  | STEP 1  Sign up     |   -- fail --> inline error, stay on page
  | Enter email address |
  +---------+-----------+
            | create unconfirmed User (email only, no password yet)
            v
  +---------------------+
  | STEP 2  Check email |   Devise confirmable — email is the ONLY way forward
  | "Confirmation sent" |   (email delivery via GOV.UK Notify; disabled locally)
  +---------+-----------+
            | user clicks confirmation link (token)
            v
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | STEP 3  Create account  (confirmation form)      |  <-- the pivotal screen
  |  - Organisation name  (dropdown, must be in      |      account + organisation + service
  |    the GOV.UK register allow-list)               |      email are ALL created here
  |  - Service email      (shared mailbox)           |
  |  - Your name                                     |
  |  - Password           (min 6 chars, zxcvbn)      |
  +---------+----------------------------------------+
            | confirm user, create Organisation + confirmed Membership, sign in
            v
  +---------------------+   MANDATORY — enforced by ApplicationController before_action
  | STEP 4  2FA setup   |   scan QR / enter secret into authenticator, verify 6-digit code
  | TOTP (RFC 6238)     |   -- wrong code --> error, stay on page
  +---------+-----------+
            | persist otp_secret_key
            v
  +-------------------------------------------------+
  | STEP 5  Invite second administrator              |  "There must be a minimum of 2
  |  - Email of team member                          |   administrators to add IPs or
  |  [ Invite as an admin ]     [ Skip for now ] ----+--+  multiple locations."
  +---------+----------------------------------------+  |  <-- SKIPPABLE at onboarding
            | invite sent                                |
            v                                            v
  +---------------------+                    +----------------------------+
  | Team members list   |                    | STEP 6  Locations / Settings|
  +---------------------+                    |  Persistent banner:         |
                                             |  "Your organisation hasn't  |
                                             |   signed the MOU yet"       |
                                             +---------+-------------------+
                                                       |
             +-----------------------------------------+-----------------+
             v                                                           v
  +---------------------------+                          +---------------------------+
  | STEP 7a  Sign the MOU      |                          | STEP 7b  Add a Location    |
  |  Who signs?                |                          |  - Address                 |
  |   o I will sign            |                          |  - Postcode                |
  |   o I will nominate someone|                          |      then  Add up to 5 IPs |
  |  > Read & Accept terms     |                          |                            |
  |    (name/email/job role +  |                          |  ! BLOCKED until: MOU signed|
  |     "I have authority" [x])|                          |    AND >= 2 administrators  |
  +---------------------------+                          +---------------------------+

   ==================================================================================
   HARD GATE (LocationsController#authorise_ip_actions): a user CANNOT add IP addresses
   or a second location until BOTH (a) the MOU is signed for the current version, and
   (b) the organisation has >= 2 administrators. These are deferred, not enforced inline
   during onboarding — the user only hits the wall when they try to add IPs.
   ==================================================================================

Ordering — who enforces it

The order is not a linear wizard; it is enforced by before_action filters and redirects:

GuardLocationEffect
authenticate_user!ApplicationControllerMust be signed in for everything except errors/sign-up.
confirm_two_factor_setupApplicationControllerSigned-in user with no TOTP → forced to Step 4 (2FA).
redirect_user_with_no_organisationApplicationControllerUser with no confirmed membership → help page.
2FA success branchtwo_factor_authentication_setup_controllerIf org lacks 2 admins → Step 5 (invite); else stored location.
HomeController#indexpost-login routerNo IPs yet → Settings/initial; else → Locations.
authorise_ip_actionsLocationsControllerBlocks IP / multi-location actions until MOU signed and ≥2 admins.

3. The journey, step by step

0Entry — Sign in

The unauthenticated entry point, linking through to "Sign up for a GovWifi administrator account".

1Sign up — enter email

A single field. The email must match a government/public-sector regexp (an allow-list read from S3; stubbed to @gov.uk in dev). No password is set yet — an unconfirmed User is created with the email only.

2Check your email

Devise confirmable: the emailed link is the only way to continue. (Delivery is via GOV.UK Notify; disabled locally.)

3Create account — the pivotal screen

Reached via the confirmation link. Here the admin sets a password and creates the organisation in one form: Organisation name (dropdown — must be in the GOV.UK organisations register allow-list), Service email ("a shared and monitored email"), Your name, and Password (min 6 chars + zxcvbn strength check). On submit the user is confirmed, the Organisation + a confirmed Membership are created, and they are signed in.

4Two-factor authentication (mandatory)

Enforced by an ApplicationController filter — a signed-in user with no TOTP cannot go anywhere else. Scan the QR / enter the secret into an authenticator, then verify a 6-digit code. A wrong code re-renders with an error.

5Invite a second administrator (skippable)

The inset states "There must be a minimum of 2 administrators to add IPs or multiple locations." and the copy explicitly allows skipping. A "Skip for now" link takes the user straight to Locations — so a single-admin org can finish onboarding.

6Locations & Settings

After skipping, the user lands on Locations with a persistent "Your organisation hasn't signed the MOU yet" banner. Settings shows the account, the organisation (service email + MOU status), and the London/Dublin RADIUS server IPs. The only "status page" is the external link on the Locations page.

7Blocking requirements — MOU & first location

The MOU is signed via a "who will sign?" choice (self or nominate someone), then a "read & accept the terms" attestation. Adding a location captures Address + Postcode; adding IPs is blocked until the MOU is signed and the org has ≥2 admins.

4. Data collected

Traced to db/schema.rb and the model files. "Journey step" = where the admin actually enters it.

Administrator — users

DataStepNotes / validation
Email address1Must match gov/public-sector regexp; unique; Devise confirmable.
Password3Length 6–80, zxcvbn strength check. Hint only says "at least 6 characters".
Name3Presence required at confirmation.
otp_secret_key (encrypted)4TOTP secret; is_super_admin default false.
Sign-in / security metadataautomaticsign-in counts/IPs, failed_attempts, locked_at, confirmation fields, invitation fields, mobile, direct_otp.

Organisation — organisations

DataStepNotes
Name3Must be in the GOV.UK organisations register allow-list; unique.
Service email3"A shared and monitored email"; validated; editable in Settings.
cba_enabledn/aSuper-admin feature flag; not collected during onboarding.

Other records

  • Membership — organisation_id, user_id, invited_by_id, invitation_token, confirmed_at, can_manage_team, can_manage_locations. First admin auto-confirmed at Step 3.
  • MOU — organisation_id, user_id, version, name, email_address, job_role. Signature is a digital attestation (checkbox + typed identity), not an uploaded document. mou_templates exists but is empty/unused.
  • Nomination — name, email, token, nominated_by, organisation_id.
  • Location — address (required), postcode (NOT NULL, UK-validated), organisation_id, auto-generated radius_secret_key. Unique per org.
  • IP — address (unique, valid IPv4), location_id (NOT NULL, cascade). Added ≤5 at a time, always under a Location.
Key data-model fact for compliance: a physical address lives on the Location, and every IP belongs to exactly one Location. There is no per-IP address field — the address is per location (a group of IPs), not per individual IP/access point.

5. Compliance comparison

#RequirementStatusEvidence
1Signed MOU✅ Met (as a gate)MOU flow exists; persistent banner nags until signed; authorise_ip_actions blocks IP/multi-location actions until signed. Digital attestation, not an uploaded doc. Surfaced only via banner/gate, not the linear flow.
2Two admins🟠 PartialThe 2-admin minimum is enforced as a functional gate, but the invite step is skippable and never completing it isn't blocked at onboarding. An invited admin isn't a confirmed admin until they accept.
3Service email✅ MetCollected at Step 3, mandatory, validated, editable, exportable by super admins. No check that the mailbox is real/monitored.
4Email verification✅ MetDevise confirmable; the confirmation link is the only path to account creation. Resend supported.
5Status page alerts🔴 Not met (in-app)No in-app status feature. resources :status is declared with no controller/view (dead code). Only an external link (status.wifi.service.gov.uk) on the Locations page. No alerting/subscription.
6Backup SSID plan🔴 Not metNo trace of "backup" or "SSID" anywhere in app/. No field, guidance or prompt.
7Physical address per IP🟠 PartialAddress is required per Location, not per IP. Every IP belongs to a located Location (address + UK postcode), but multiple IPs share one address and there is no per-access-point address.

Summary: 3 met, 3 partial, 1 clearly not met (plus status-alerts not met in-app). Strongest: email verification and service email (baked into account creation). Weakest: missing status-alerts and backup-SSID concepts, and "two admins" being deferrable rather than guaranteed.

6. Recommendations

R1 — Make "two admins" a tracked commitment, not a silent skip

Keep the skip, but add a persistent dashboard banner ("Add a second administrator — 1 of 2") like the MOU banner, email a reminder after N days, show it as an onboarding checklist item that stays incomplete, and distinguish invited vs confirmed.

R2 — Add a first-class status page / alerts capability

Remove or implement the dead resources :status route. Recommended: an in-app Service status panel pulling from status.wifi.service.gov.uk, plus opt-in incident alerts during onboarding (reuse the verified service email). Closes requirement #5.

R3 — Introduce a "Backup SSID / resilience plan" step

A short onboarding screen (or Settings card) capturing the fallback plan if GovWifi is unavailable — backup SSID name, owner, a confirmation checkbox. Closes requirement #6.

R4 — Turn onboarding into an explicit checklist / task list

Replace the chain of redirects + banners with a GOV.UK task-list landing page so the sequence is legible and every compliance item is visible up front. See wireframe W1.

R5 — Bring the MOU into the onboarding flow

Offer the MOU as a checklist item right after the 2nd-admin step, so both blocking requirements are handled before the user tries — and fails — to add IPs.

R6 — Clarify the password rule

The field enforces zxcvbn strength but the hint only says "at least 6 characters". Show the real rule and give inline strength feedback.

R7 — Consider per-access-point address granularity

Either allow an optional address/label per IP, or document that location-level address is the accepted control. At minimum prompt for a location name/label so multi-IP sites are intelligible.

R8 — Verify the service email is monitored

Optionally send a one-time confirmation to the service mailbox so "shared and monitored" is more than a hint.

R9 — Fix the local-dev make serve target (podman)

make stop runs docker compose … rm -fsv, which podman-compose rejects (invalid choice: 'rm'), aborting make serve. Replace with down -v --remove-orphans.

7. Low-fidelity wireframes

W1 — Onboarding task list (new landing after 2FA)

Implements R4/R5/R1/R3 — one legible page that makes every compliance item visible.

+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| GOV.UK  GovWifi                                 Support . Sign out|
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Cabinet Office . Switch organisation                              |
|                                                                   |
|  Set up GovWifi for your organisation                             |
|  Complete these steps before you can add access points.           |
|                                                                   |
|  Applied for                                                      |
|  --------------------------------------------------------------   |
|  1. Create your admin account ......................... [x] Done  |
|  2. Set up two-factor authentication .................. [x] Done  |
|                                                                   |
|  Make your organisation compliant                                 |
|  --------------------------------------------------------------   |
|  3. Add a second administrator ................ [ To do ]     (!) |
|        1 of 2 administrators added                                |
|  4. Sign the Memorandum of Understanding ...... [ To do ]     (!) |
|  5. Add a backup SSID / resilience plan ....... [ To do ]         |
|  6. Opt in to service status alerts ........... [ To do ]         |
|                                                                   |
|  Start using GovWifi                                              |
|  --------------------------------------------------------------   |
|  7. Add your first location and IP addresses .. [Cannot           |
|        Complete steps 3 and 4 first          start yet]           |
|                                                                   |
|  Your progress: 2 of 7 steps complete                             |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+

W2 — Second-admin step with persistent tracking

Implements R1 — keep the skip, but keep the outstanding requirement visible afterwards.

+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  (!) Important                                                    |
|  You have 1 of 2 required administrators. Add a second admin      |
|  before you can add access points.               [ Add admin ]   |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Invite a team member to be an administrator                      |
|                                                                   |
|  Two admins are required so you can recover access if you lose    |
|  your 2FA device, and so there is always a contact if someone     |
|  leaves.                                                          |
|                                                                   |
|  Email address of team member                                    |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|  |                                           |                    |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|                                                                   |
|  [ Invite as an admin ]                                           |
|                                                                   |
|  Skip for now  ->  (a reminder banner stays until this is done)   |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+

W3 — Service status & alerts (closes requirement #5)

Implements R2 — a real in-app status surface + opt-in alerts, reusing the verified service email.

+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Service status                                                   |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|  |  * All systems operational        (from status.wifi...)     |   |
|  |  RADIUS London   * OK      RADIUS Dublin   * OK             |   |
|  |  Last checked: 2 minutes ago          View full status ->  |   |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|                                                                   |
|  Incident alerts                                                  |
|  Get emailed when there is a GovWifi incident or maintenance      |
|                                                                   |
|  Send alerts to:  govwifi-team@gov.uk  (your service email)      |
|  [x] Email me about incidents                                     |
|  [x] Email me about planned maintenance                           |
|  [ Save alert preferences ]                                       |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+

W4 — Backup SSID / resilience plan (closes requirement #6)

Implements R3 — a lightweight capture of the organisation's fallback plan.

+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Your backup connectivity plan                                    |
|                                                                   |
|  Tell us what your organisation will use if GovWifi is            |
|  unavailable. This helps us support you during an incident.       |
|                                                                   |
|  Backup SSID name (optional)                                      |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|  |                                           |                    |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|                                                                   |
|  Who owns the backup network?                                     |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|  |                                           |                    |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|                                                                   |
|  [ ] We have a documented fallback plan for wifi outages          |
|                                                                   |
|  [ Save ]      Skip - I don't have a backup plan yet              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+

W5 — Location with per-IP labelling (addresses requirement #7)

Implements R7 — keep address at location level but give each IP a name/label.

+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  Add a location                                                   |
|                                                                   |
|  Location name                                                    |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|  | Head office - 3rd floor                   |                    |
|  +-------------------------------------------+                    |
|  Address                          Postcode                        |
|  +-----------------------------+  +--------------+                |
|  | 10 High Street, London      |  | SW1A 1AA     |                |
|  +-----------------------------+  +--------------+                |
|                                                                   |
|  IP addresses at this location (up to 5)                          |
|  +-------------------+  Label (optional)                          |
|  | 203.0.113.10      |  +------------------------+                |
|  +-------------------+  | Reception AP           |                |
|  +-------------------+  +------------------------+                |
|  | 203.0.113.11      |  | Meeting rooms AP       |                |
|  +-------------------+  +------------------------+                |
|                                                                   |
|  [ Save location and IPs ]                                        |

Generated locally from a live walkthrough of govwifi-admin. Screenshots embedded as base64; no external files required.
